ops.text: E8 ROUND 1 (cell a, local Qwen3-4B on render-v2 data) JUDGED: E8(a) dominates v001 on every hard metric — abstain F1 0.717 vs 0.160, option-resolution 0.362 vs 0.021, val AUC 0.9008 (best ckpt iter1250, budget thr 0.2871 @ R 0.658/1.2% false-fire vs v001 R 0.360), attractor share 0.018 vs E2's 0.55 (mode collapse eliminated by render-v2 + dedup + corrective oversampling), grounding gap +0.14. BUT the promotion gate (corrective same-direction with non-overlapping CIs) reads TIE: E8 0.125 [0, 0.375] vs v001 0.000 [0,0] — v001 barely fires on v2 prompts (fire 0.062; it is out-of-distribution there) and E8's fired-corrective n (~16) leaves the CI floor at 0. Per the approved tie rule the incumbent STAYS; daemon remains self-consistent on v1 rendering. Judge controls passed both arms (garbage-loss 1.0). NEXT: cells b/c/d launched on Tinker/Qwen3-8B (rank16 + rank32, 2608 steps ≈ 2 epochs, ~$6.5 each, all-36-layer LoRA inherent to Tinker) — winner re-enters the gate; if corrective-n stays too thin for non-overlap, widen the judged corrective stratum before deciding.
